title: Review: add secretx-k8s backend
notes:
Maintainer risk: MEDIUM — Two P0 bugs (non-Zeroizing byte copy; README wrong API contract), one P1 publish-blocker (Status:Planned in README). Nothing data-lossy in the happy path but secret bytes linger in non-Zeroizing memory after put() which violates project invariant.
Confidence: 15% chance of missed bug. Least confident flags: (1) kube v3 API surface — is_forbidden() existence unverified, SSA serialization of apiVersion/kind via k8s-openapi implicit Serialize; (2) e2e test race conditions depend on cargo test process isolation semantics. Would raise confidence with: a full cargo test --all-features run; kube source review for ErrorResponse::is_forbidden; confirmed k8s-openapi Serialize for apiVersion/kind in Patch::Apply.
Idiom rating: ACCEPTABLE — Core idiom issues are mechanical (to_string on literal, use inside fn, into_values). One real P1: .unwrap() in production get() path. No unsafe, no Box<dyn Error> in API, no lifetime issues, no gratuitous clones beyond those required by ByteString ownership. async-trait could be removed at the secretx-core level but that's not secretx-k8s scope.
Rust idiom confidence: 10% chance of missed issue. Least confident flags: whether kube ErrorResponse::is_forbidden() exists in kube v3 (unable to verify without kube source). Uncertainty driven by: not being able to cargo check the code live to catch compile errors.
